title: Single Amplified Genome Assembly
before_start:

steps: Run pre-QC (SGA) cd $HOME#start a script session to keep a record of your workscript# Set up a working directory.export ASSEMBLY=${HOME}/assembly #or whatever path you like...mkdir -p $ASSEMBLY# IMPORTANT: First setup your environmentexport PATH=/path/to/bin/required_software:${PATH}# Link the datamkdir -p $ASSEMBLY/datacd $ASSEMBLY/dataln -s /path/to/data/sd-mda/*/*fastq.gz .ln -s /path/to/data/mda/*fastq.gz .# Let's start by assembling an MDA amplified E. coli cell# First let's see what the data looks likemodule purgemodule load gcc/4.8.2 # NOTE that does not work with 4.9.2module load PYTHON/2.7.3 # required for several accompanying scriptsmodule load SGA/0.10.13mkdir -p $ASSEMBLY/sags/preqccd $ASSEMBLY/sags/preqcsga preprocess --phred64 --pe-mode 1 $ASSEMBLY/data/ecoli_mda.1.fastq.gz $ASSEMBLY/data/ecoli_mda.2.fastq.gz > ecoli_mda.preqc.fastqsga index -a ropebwt --no-reverse -t 2 ecoli_mda.preqc.fastqsga preqc -t 2 --force-EM ecoli_mda.preqc.fastq > ecoli_mda.preqcsga-preqc-report.py -o ecoli_mda_sga_plots --page_per_plot ecoli_mda.preqc cd $HOME #start a script session to keep a record of your work script # Set up a working directory. export ASSEMBLY=${HOME}/assembly #or whatever path you like... mkdir -p $ASSEMBLY # IMPORTANT: First setup your environment export PATH=/path/to/bin/required_software:${PATH} # Link the data mkdir -p $ASSEMBLY/data cd $ASSEMBLY/data ln -s /path/to/data/sd-mda/*/*fastq.gz . ln -s /path/to/data/mda/*fastq.gz . # Let's start by assembling an MDA amplified E. coli cell # First let's see what the data looks like module purge module load gcc/4.8.2 # NOTE that does not work with 4.9.2 module load PYTHON/2.7.3 # required for several accompanying scripts module load SGA/0.10.13 mkdir -p $ASSEMBLY/sags/preqc cd $ASSEMBLY/sags/preqc sga preprocess --phred64 --pe-mode 1 $ASSEMBLY/data/ecoli_mda.1.fastq.gz $ASSEMBLY/data/ecoli_mda.2.fastq.gz > ecoli_mda.preqc.fastq sga index -a ropebwt --no-reverse -t 2 ecoli_mda.preqc.fastq sga preqc -t 2 --force-EM ecoli_mda.preqc.fastq > ecoli_mda.preqc sga-preqc-report.py -o ecoli_mda_sga_plots --page_per_plot ecoli_mda.preqc cat ecoli_mda.preqc | perl -e 'while(<>){if (/FragmentSize/){my $line = ;while(my $line=<>){if($line=~m/(\d+)/){print '$1\n';}else{exit;}}}}' > ecoli_mda.fragsizesmodule load RR#> frag summary(frag)# V1# Min. : 52.0# 1st Qu.: 257.0# Median : 281.0# Mean : 259.9# 3rd Qu.: 301.0# Max. :1246.0#> quit() Check insert size in R cat ecoli_mda.preqc | perl -e 'while(<>){if (/FragmentSize/){my $line = ;while(my $line=<>){if($line=~m/(\d+)/){print '$1\n';}else{exit;}}}}' > ecoli_mda.fragsizes module load R R #> frag<-read.table('ecoli_mda.fragsizes',header=F) #> summary(frag) # V1 # Min. : 52.0 # 1st Qu.: 257.0 # Median : 281.0 # Mean : 259.9 # 3rd Qu.: 301.0 # Max. :1246.0 #> quit() Check insert size in R SPAdes E. coli (single cell)
Adapter and Quality Trim (trim_galore) trim_galore --gzip -q 10 --paired --retain_unpaired reads.1.fastq.gz reads.2.fastq.gz Trim Illumina adapters and quality trim 3' bases less than Phred Q10

Determine composition / Identify genome if known (Kraken) if not already know from 16S RNA sequencing. kraken --db /path/to/kraken_db/kraken_abfpv_21_08_2016 --threads 4 --preload --fastq-input --gzip-compressed --paired --quick --only-classified-output --output ecoli_sdmda.kraken.out $ASSEMBLY/data/ecoli_sdmda.100k.1.fastq.gz $ASSEMBLY/data/ecoli_sdmda.100k.2.fastq.gz

Run SPAdes (optionally run ccSAG to do co-assembly of SAGs -- includes QC step 2) SPAdes 3.11.1 Linux http://cab.spbu.ru/software/spades/ mkdir -p $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_ecoli_mdacd $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_ecoli_mda# IMPORTANT! make sure to turn on the single-cell mode with --sc# Optionally add --careful# Loading the environment with module load in the job script itself ensures the proper optimizations are used depending on which partition the job is run.module purge; module load gcc/4.9.2 SPADES/3.5.0spades.py --only-assembler --sc -1 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/ecoli_mda.1.fastq.gz -2 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/ecoli_mda.2.fastq.gz -t 4 -o ecoli_mda_spadesmkdir -p $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_ecoli_sdmdacd $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_ecoli_sdmdamodule purge; module load gcc/4.9.2 SPADES/3.5.0spades.py --sc -1 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/ecoli_sdmda.1.fastq.gz -2 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/ecoli_sdmda.2.fastq.gz -t 24 -o ecoli_sdmda_spadesmkdir -p $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_bsubtilis_sdmdacd $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_bsubtilis_sdmdamodule purge; module load gcc/4.9.2 SPADES/3.5.0spades.py --sc -1 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/bsubtilis_sdmda.1.fastq.gz -2 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/bsubtilis_sdmda.2.fastq.gz -t 24 -o bsubtilis_sdmda_spadesmkdir -p $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_soil_sdmdacd $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_soil_sdmdamodule purge; module load gcc/4.9.2 SPADES/3.5.0spades.py --sc -1 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/soil_sdmda.1.fastq.gz -2 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/soil_sdmda.2.fastq.gz -t 24 -o soil_sdmda_spades mkdir -p $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_ecoli_mda cd $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_ecoli_mda # IMPORTANT! make sure to turn on the single-cell mode with --sc # Optionally add --careful # Loading the environment with module load in the job script itself ensures the proper optimizations are used depending on which partition the job is run. module purge; module load gcc/4.9.2 SPADES/3.5.0 spades.py --only-assembler --sc -1 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/ecoli_mda.1.fastq.gz -2 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/ecoli_mda.2.fastq.gz -t 4 -o ecoli_mda_spades mkdir -p $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_ecoli_sdmda cd $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_ecoli_sdmda module purge; module load gcc/4.9.2 SPADES/3.5.0 spades.py --sc -1 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/ecoli_sdmda.1.fastq.gz -2 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/ecoli_sdmda.2.fastq.gz -t 24 -o ecoli_sdmda_spades mkdir -p $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_bsubtilis_sdmda cd $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_bsubtilis_sdmda module purge; module load gcc/4.9.2 SPADES/3.5.0 spades.py --sc -1 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/bsubtilis_sdmda.1.fastq.gz -2 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/bsubtilis_sdmda.2.fastq.gz -t 24 -o bsubtilis_sdmda_spades mkdir -p $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_soil_sdmda cd $ASSEMBLY/sags/spades_soil_sdmda module purge; module load gcc/4.9.2 SPADES/3.5.0 spades.py --sc -1 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/soil_sdmda.1.fastq.gz -2 ${ASSEMBLY}/data/soil_sdmda.2.fastq.gz -t 24 -o soil_sdmda_spades ccSAG Linux https://github.com/mstkgw/ccSAG

Determine completeness (CheckM and/or BUSCO) ### BUSCO is used to determine genome-completeness based on looking for conserved genes that should be presentmkdir $ASSEMBLY/sags/BUSCOln -s /path/to/spades_ecoli_mda/ecoli_mda_spades/scaffolds.fasta ecoli_mda_spades.fasta# Check AUGUSTUS installation directory for Augustus parameter file# Copy the augustus config directory (/project/devel/aateam/bin/augustus-3.0.2/config/) somewhere local and set the export AUGUSTUS_CONFIG_PATH=/path/to/new/config/module purge; module load gcc/4.9.3-gold; module load python/3.5.1; module load hmmer; module load boost/1.55.0export AUGUSTUS_CONFIG_PATH=/path/to/augustus-3.0.2/config/python3 /path/to/busco-3.0.2/scripts/run_BUSCO.py -o busco_ecoli_mda_spades -i ecoli_mda_spades.fasta -l proteobacteria_odb9 -m genome -c 8 -sp E_coli_K12 -f BUSCO ### BUSCO is used to determine genome-completeness based on looking for conserved genes that should be present mkdir $ASSEMBLY/sags/BUSCO ln -s /path/to/spades_ecoli_mda/ecoli_mda_spades/scaffolds.fasta ecoli_mda_spades.fasta # Check AUGUSTUS installation directory for Augustus parameter file # Copy the augustus config directory (/project/devel/aateam/bin/augustus-3.0.2/config/) somewhere local and set the export AUGUSTUS_CONFIG_PATH=/path/to/new/config/ module purge; module load gcc/4.9.3-gold; module load python/3.5.1; module load hmmer; module load boost/1.55.0 export AUGUSTUS_CONFIG_PATH=/path/to/augustus-3.0.2/config/ python3 /path/to/busco-3.0.2/scripts/run_BUSCO.py -o busco_ecoli_mda_spades -i ecoli_mda_spades.fasta -l proteobacteria_odb9 -m genome -c 8 -sp E_coli_K12 -f BUSCO # Assume you have putative genomes in the directory /home/donovan/bins with fa as the file extension and want to store the CheckM results in /home/donovan/checkm. To processes these genomes with 8 threads, simply run:checkm lineage_wf -t 8 -x fa /home/donovan/bins /home/donovan/checkm CheckM # Assume you have putative genomes in the directory /home/donovan/bins with fa as the file extension and want to store the CheckM results in /home/donovan/checkm. To processes these genomes with 8 threads, simply run: checkm lineage_wf -t 8 -x fa /home/donovan/bins /home/donovan/checkm CheckM

(Optional) Align to Reference (MUMMER) Mummer Linux https://github.com/mummer4/mummer dnadiff -p query.v.ref ecoli_ref_NC_000913.3.fasta ecoli_mda_spades.fastamummerplot --png out.delta dnadiff -p query.v.ref ecoli_ref_NC_000913.3.fasta ecoli_mda_spades.fasta mummerplot --png out.delta
Download datasets (or generate them if doing a real experiment!) SPAdes E. coli (single cell) sd-MDA E. coli reference genome
creator: Tyler Alioto
